name: pennylane
description: Cross-platform Python library for quantum computing, quantum machine learning, and quantum chemistry. Enables building and training quantum circuits with automatic differentiation, seamless integration with PyTorch/JAX/TensorFlow, and device-independent execution across simulators and quantum hardware (IBM, Amazon Braket, Google, Rigetti, IonQ, etc.). Use when working with quantum circuits, variational quantum algorithms (VQE, QAOA), quantum neural networks, hybrid quantum-classical models, molecular simulations, quantum chemistry calculations, or any quantum computing tasks requiring gradient-based optimization, hardware-agnostic programming, or quantum machine learning workflows.
capabilities: Build quantum circuits with single and multi-qubit gates
Train variational quantum algorithms with automatic differentiation
Execute circuits on simulators or quantum hardware
Integrate with PyTorch, JAX, and TensorFlow workflows
Compute molecular Hamiltonians for quantum chemistry
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing educational Python code examples for quantum computing workflows. All 419 static findings are false positives: code examples in markdown files are documentation, not executable code. The skill has no scripts, no network calls, no file system access, and no real credentials. The api_key placeholders are documentation examples, not actual secrets. Quantum computing algorithms flagged as 'weak crypto' are legitimate scientific computing patterns.
valueStatement: Quantum computing tasks require specialized knowledge of circuit design and optimization. PennyLane provides a unified interface for building, training, and executing quantum circuits with automatic differentiation across simulators and real hardware.
limitations: Quantum hardware access requires additional plugins
Circuit size limited by simulator resources
Gradient-based training may face barren plateaus

useCases.title: Quantum chemistry simulations
useCases.description: Calculate molecular ground state energies using VQE with UCCSD ansatz for drug discovery research

useCases.title: Hybrid quantum-classical models
useCases.description: Build variational classifiers combining quantum circuits with PyTorch or TensorFlow layers

useCases.title: Optimization algorithms
useCases.description: Implement QAOA for combinatorial optimization problems like MaxCut on graphs

promptTemplates.title: Basic circuit
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a 2-qubit quantum circuit in PennyLane that applies RX and RY rotations then measures PauliZ on qubit 0

promptTemplates.title: VQE calculation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a PennyLane script to compute the ground state energy of H2 molecule using VQE with Adam optimizer

promptTemplates.title: Hybrid classifier
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a hybrid quantum-classical classifier in PennyLane that integrates with PyTorch for training on a dataset

promptTemplates.title: Hardware execution
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to execute a PennyLane circuit on IBM Quantum hardware using the qiskit.ibmq device with parameter-shift gradients

bestPractices: Start with simulators before deploying to quantum hardware
Use parameter-shift rule for gradient computation on hardware
Initialize parameters carefully to avoid barren plateaus
Cache device objects to reduce initialization overhead
antiPatterns: Using backpropagation on quantum hardware (only works on simulators)
Skipping circuit validation on simulators before hardware execution
Using too many qubits beyond simulator capacity
Ignoring noise models when simulating hardware behavior

faq.question: What quantum hardware does PennyLane support?
faq.answer: IBM Quantum, Amazon Braket, Google Cirq, Rigetti, IonQ, and Azure Quantum through device plugins.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of qubits for simulation?
faq.answer: default.qubit handles about 25 qubits; lightning.qubit extends to about 30 qubits depending on available memory.

faq.question: How does PennyLane integrate with PyTorch?
faq.answer: Use interface='torch' in QNode decorator; PennyLane tensors are compatible with torch.autograd for backpropagation.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using cloud quantum hardware?
faq.answer: Circuit definitions are sent to hardware providers; sensitive data should be minimized in circuit parameters.

faq.question: Why are my gradients vanishing in deep circuits?
faq.answer: Barren plateaus affect deep circuits; try initialization strategies, shallower circuits, or local cost functions.

faq.question: How does PennyLane compare to Qiskit or Cirq?
faq.answer: PennyLane focuses on variational algorithms and automatic differentiation; Qiskit and Cirq are lower-level frameworks.
